title: The Art of Firewatch
creator: Jane Ng
description: Environment artist Jane Ng explains how a very small team built Firewatch’s stylized Wyoming wilderness, from visual goals and terrain tools to trees, props, lighting, and production tradeoffs.
useful: It shows how art direction becomes a feasible production plan when a small team cannot solve every asset by hand.
audience: Environment artists, art directors, and small teams planning a cohesive 3D world.
learning: Turn a visual target into production constraints
Combine procedural terrain with authored detail
Prioritize tools and assets for a small art team
